unexpected.label: shallow-N j=0 cell shift (op:shallow-j-effect-d4)
unexpected.description: Extended-window enumeration produced 99 NEW j=0 quartics (a3..a0 in [-5..5], a4 in {1,2,3,5,7}, primitive, irreducible, non-singular, not in Q1's 60). Their delta_R13_free distribution is shifted toward zero relative to the Q1 non-j=0 cluster (median -2.49e-3 vs -3.73e-3). The shift survives stratification on coefficient sum |a3|+|a2|+|a1|+|a0| within the Q1 band [6,12] (n=77 stratified, Mann-Whitney U=3381, p=1.1e-6).
unexpected.deep_N_status: Does NOT survive to deep-N: family 32 deep-WKB delta = -4.55e-4 matches non-j=0 baseline fam01 deep delta = -4.60e-4 (diff 0.24 pooled stderr).
unexpected.interpretation: Real shallow-N statistical effect, plausibly a beta*log(N) or gamma absorption term that loads partly on the j stratum at small N. Does not extend to a deep-N rule. Recorded in v1.3 as Remark, with op:shallow-j-effect-d4 added to Open Problems.

unexpected.label: fixed-A residualization residual_mean is structurally zero (methodology footgun)
unexpected.description: With a free intercept gamma in the regression, residual_mean is forced to zero by least-squares. We initially planned to use residual_mean as the primary diagnostic; in practice residual_at_max_n (extrapolated tail residual) and the FREE-A delta are the meaningful summaries. Documented in rubber_duck_critique.md (focus item iii).
unexpected.fix: Phase R13-A and R13-B used residual_at_max_n and FREE-A delta as primary diagnostics; residual_mean is reported but downweighted. The d=3 sanity PASSES via residual_at_max_n (rho=+0.55, Bonf p=1.1e-4) and via delta_R13_free (rho=-0.57, Bonf p=5e-5). residual_mean rho=-0.20 is uninformative.

unexpected.label: deep-WKB shrinks fam32 |delta_4| by ~8x (uniform sub-leading systematic decays)
unexpected.description: Family 32 |delta_4| = 3.71e-3 at shallow N=250 (R13-B) -> 4.55e-4 at deep N=800 (R13-D). Decay rate ~ N^{-1.7}, neither 1/N nor 1/log^2 N. Family 1 (non-j=0 baseline) shrinks at the same rate to 4.60e-4. This documents that the 'uniform delta_4 ~ -3.5e-3 across all 60 Q1 quartics' observed in R1.2 was indeed a fit-window artefact, but BOTH j=0 and non-j=0 families are subject to the same universal sub-leading WKB systematic; the j=0 cell does NOT separate.

unexpected.label: 8 D_4 j=0 quartics in extended cell (op:galois-modular-stratification)
unexpected.description: Of the 99 NEW j=0 quartics enumerated at W=5, 91 have Galois group S_4 and 8 have D_4 (cands 22, 23, 34, 35, 60, 67, 22-paired). No V_4, C_4, A_4 j=0 quartics found in this window. Whether the D_4 sub-cell has distinct WKB behavior is unexamined (would need deep-WKB on each D_4 family). Recorded as a non-halt flag for v1.4.
